summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments involving small UAS. A Ukrainian firm has developed the "Chipa" net gun to physically snare FPV drones when other air defenses fail, while Ukrainian engineers also created a net gun to counter FPV drones. Ukrainian FPV drones struck Russian logistics 50 km from the front line in Donetsk and 50 km behind the front in the same region. Russia expanded its drone force to 101,000 as Ukraine accelerates FPV drone development on the front lines. An FPV drone slammed into a U.S. military base in Iraq, and Iranian FPV drones struck the same U.S. air base while Trump rejected aid to Ukraine. Russian FPV drones struck a Slatyne store, injuring two men, and hit a worker bus in Mykolaiv Oblast, injuring two. Russian forces struck Rozumivka with an FPV drone, wounding two civilians, and a Russian FPV drone killed two medics in Kharkiv region. Residents in the Kharkov region cut fiber-optic cables of Ukrainian FPV drones.
